What Was Done: Fixed Python 3.9 compatibility issues across media CRUD, media service, media endpoint, platform registry, and OAuth-related files
Outcome / Result: Backend booted cleanly without Python union-type runtime errors

What Was Done: Added missing runtime packages like `python-multipart` and verified media upload dependencies
Outcome / Result: Swagger and multipart routes became loadable after dependency fixes
Notes: Rebuild was required because dependency files changed

What Was Done: Created and fixed login/callback routes for Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Google/YouTube, and Twitter
Outcome / Result: Route registration completed and login redirects started working
Notes: YouTube uses Google OAuth route, not a separate `/youtube/login`

What Was Done: Established faster local workflow using Docker restart for Python code changes and rebuild only for dependency/image changes
Outcome / Result: Local iteration became faster and less disruptive
Notes: Documented when to use restart vs rebuild

What Was Done: Pushed backend code to GitHub and prepared project for Vercel / hosted frontend integration
Notes: Force-push was used because remote had unrelated old contents

What Was Done: Built simple Next.js frontend with dashboard, platform connect buttons, universal composer, media upload flow, and scheduled posts view
Outcome / Result: Frontend became usable locally and on Vercel
Notes: UI still needed improvement for platform-specific metadata inputs

What Was Done: Reworked composer so users enter normal platform-specific inputs instead of raw JSON and fixed YouTube login mapping
Outcome / Result: Frontend became more professional and easier to use
Notes: Backend still receives JSONB, but frontend now assembles it internally

What Was Done: Implemented publish-now, cancel, scheduled dispatch, and worker-driven status updates
Outcome / Result: Immediate, scheduled, and cancelled post flows now work locally
Notes: Publisher is still a simulated publish step, not real provider publishing

What Was Done: Debugged worker task registration, import cycles, datetime scheduling issues, and publisher transaction behavior
Outcome / Result: Celery now receives tasks and marks posts posted / cancelled correctly
Notes: Had to rebuild worker/beat because they do not live-mount source like backend

What Was Done: Implemented Phase 1 text-based RLS, added session tenant context, added `tenant_id` to `post_media`, and applied Alembic migration
Outcome / Result: DB and API both now enforce tenant isolation for current string tenant IDs
Notes: RLS is only meaningful when app connects with `social_manager`, not `postgres`

What Was Done: Validated tenant isolation with `tenant_123` and `tenant_999` both from SQL and from FastAPI endpoints
Notes: This is the current baseline for senior review
